title: Brain App Manifest Schema
description: Frontmatter schema for brain-app.yaml — the manifest declared by an installable brain app. A brain app runs as a sandboxed iframe inside a BrainFoundry brain, addressing brain memory and endpoints through a postMessage bridge gated by manifest-declared permissions. Validates the YAML manifest at the root of any installable brain-app repo.

dialect.description: Schema dialect this manifest conforms to. Bumped when the schema changes incompatibly.

id.description: Kebab-case identifier for this app. Must be unique within a brain's installed apps. Used as the local mount id (URL path /apps/<id>/, app dir brain-apps/<id>/).

name.description: Human-readable app name. May contain dots and capitals (e.g., 'hbar.ink').

version.description: Semver. Bumped on each release. The brain pins a specific commit SHA at install; the version is informational.

description.description: One-line description. Shown to operator on the install approval screen.

license.description: SPDX license identifier (e.g., 'AGPL-3.0', 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0'). Apps installed into a brain must be open-source-compatible with the brain's own license.

author.description: Who wrote the app. Shown to operator on install approval screen.

repo.description: Canonical git repository URL the brain clones from on install. Currently restricted to public-readable forges. Federation/private-repo install is a v2 question.

label.description: Text shown in the brain nav.

route.description: URL path the tab links to in the brain UI. Must not collide with built-in routes (/, /chat, /upload, /federation, /trace, /settings, /update, /future). Collision is a runtime install-time check, not a schema check.

order.description: Sort key in the nav. Built-ins use 10/20/30/...; installed apps default to 100. Lower numbers render first.

tab.description: How this app appears in the brain UI nav.

ui_bundle.description: Path (relative to manifest) to a pre-built static bundle directory. Must contain index.html. Served by the brain at /apps/<id>/. Pre-built (not built-at-install) because brain hosts are RAM-constrained.

api.description: Path (relative to manifest) to a Python module exporting an APIRouter named 'router'. Mounted by the brain at /apps/<id>/api/. Inherits brain's Python deps; do NOT bundle conflicting versions.

ui_url.description: Dev-mode escape hatch. If set AND the brain is running with BRAIN_DEV_MODE=true, the iframe points here instead of the bundle. Lets app authors iterate without rebuilding on every change.

entries.description: Where the runtime artifacts live in the app repo. At least one of ui_bundle or api must be set; an api-only app has no tab UI and contributes only endpoints (rare; usually paired with another app).

requires_layers.description: Brain memory layers this app reads or writes. Operator approves on install. Empty/absent = app cannot touch memory through the bridge.

layer.description: Memory layer per the cognitive-architecture taxonomy (D37). Episodic = events/drops; semantic = stable facts; procedural = how-to/skills.

mode.description: read = query only; write = create/update/delete; append = write-only without overwrite (audit-friendly default for drops).

requires_endpoints.description: Brain core HTTP endpoints this app calls through the bridge. Informational in v0; in v1 the bridge enforces this as an allowlist.

permissions.description: Coarse permission tokens the app must hold to be useful. Operator approves the full set on install; uninstall revokes all. v0 enforcement is server-side at the bridge.

brainfoundry-nous.description: Semver range (e.g., '>=0.6.0', '^0.7.0'). Install fails if the brain's brainfoundry-nous version does not satisfy this range.

engines.description: Compatibility requirements. brainfoundry-nous version is the only engine in v1.

then.description: If the app declares memory.write, it MUST declare which layers (with mode write or append) so the operator sees the scope on install.
If the app declares memory.read, it MUST declare which layers (with mode read) so the operator sees the scope on install.
